title: Death rate from unsafe sex
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to [unsafe sex](#dod:unsafe_sex) per 100,000 people. Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.
note: To allow for comparisons between countries and over time, this metric is [age-standardized](#dod:age_standardized).
caption:
Death rate from unsafe sex

Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex per 100,000 people. Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.

This choropleth world map displays 2023 death rates from unsafe sex per 100,000 people, using an age-standardized metric to allow for comparisons between countries and over time. The legend ranges from 0 to 300, with specific thresholds marked at 3, 10, 30, and 100. The highest visible rates, falling into the darkest red band above 100 and extending toward 300, are concentrated in Southern Africa, where South Africa, Botswana, and Zimbabwe sit in the most intense shades. Surrounding countries in Eastern and Central Africa, including Tanzania, Zambia, and Mozambique, appear in slightly lighter but still dark orange-red bands that fall between 30 and 100. A broad swath of West Africa shows mid-range orange shades, generally between 10 and 30. In contrast, North America, most of Europe, Australia, and large parts of Asia sit predominantly in the lightest shades of the legend, near or below the 3 threshold. Latin America displays a mix of light to mid-range shades, with no country reaching the darkest bands. Greenland and a few small island territories show either a very pale shade or hatching; the exact status is difficult to distinguish. The visual pattern shows the highest death rates clustered in Southern and parts of Eastern Africa, with progressively lighter shades distributed across the rest of the world.
